title: Android APK packaging (Capacitor) for the expense tracker frontend

requirements.summary: Add Android packaging support (Capacitor) so the existing React app can be built into an installable Android APK and render all existing routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Repo includes Android packaging configuration and an Android project scaffold suitable for building an APK from the existing frontend.
A developer can run documented commands to build a debug APK locally (or in CI) without manual Android Studio project setup beyond installing Android SDK prerequisites.
The Android app loads the deployed Internet Computer frontend URL or the bundled web assets and renders the existing routes (/ dashboard, /add, /expenses, /settings) without blank screens.

file_operations.description: Add Capacitor configuration for the app (appId/appName, webDir=dist) with support for either bundled web assets or loading a remote IC frontend URL via an environment-driven setting. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Commit a Capacitor-generated Android app manifest configured for network access and WebView behavior suitable for the React SPA (including navigation for internal routes). Ensure any required permissions and intent filters for WebView/deep-linking are present. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Commit a Capacitor-generated MainActivity with any minimal WebView settings needed to prevent blank screens and support SPA navigation under a WebView wrapper (Capacitor standard approach). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add Android network security configuration for development (if needed) and to allow required IC/identity domains when using remote URL mode. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
Add the Capacitor Android scaffold gradle variables file (as generated by Capacitor) to pin SDK versions for reproducible builds. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Integrate a small Capacitor runtime initializer (import and run) so the SPA behaves correctly inside an Android WebView wrapper (e.g., handle external navigation and avoid blank screens for internal routes). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a utility that detects Capacitor runtime and applies WebView-safe behaviors for SPA routing and external navigation (high-level glue only; do not modify immutable auth hooks). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Make Internet Identity login work reliably in the Android WebView wrapper by handling WebView navigation/allow-list behavior and documenting any user steps.
acceptanceCriteria: From the Android APK, a user can initiate login via Internet Identity and return to the app in an authenticated state.
Expense CRUD operations still work from the Android APK after authentication (add, edit, delete, list).
Any required Android network/navigation configuration (e.g., allowing identity and canister domains) is included in the repo and described in documentation.

file_operations.description: Add a Capacitor-specific auth bridge that makes Internet Identity’s popup/redirect behavior work in an Android WebView context by coordinating external navigation (when necessary) and return-to-app handling, without modifying frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x) or frontend/src/main.tsx. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Wire the Capacitor auth bridge initialization at app startup (within AppShell) so II login can complete and the user returns to the SPA authenticated when running inside the APK. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Adjust Android intent filters / navigation handling needed for Internet Identity return-to-app behavior (deep link/custom scheme as required by the chosen Capacitor approach) and ensure required domains are reachable. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Update allow-list/network security rules to ensure the APK can reach Internet Identity and the target IC domains used by the app (remote URL mode and/or identity flows), matching documentation. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Document Internet Identity behavior on Android (what the user will see during login, any required Android prompts/settings, and how the app returns to authenticated state), plus troubleshooting steps for WebView navigation issues. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Add in-repo developer documentation for building, signing (optional), and installing the Android APK (debug and optional release).
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation exists in-repo (e.g., README section) with step-by-step instructions to generate an APK.
Docs specify how to install the APK onto a physical Android device (ADB install) and any Android settings needed (e.g., allow unknown sources).
Docs explain how the APK points to the app (bundled assets vs remote URL) and how to change the target URL if needed.

file_operations.description: Add a dedicated section describing prerequisites (Android SDK/Java), commands to build the web assets and sync to Android, Gradle commands to produce a debug APK, where the APK artifact is output, and ADB install steps (including enabling unknown sources). Include guidance for optional release signing and how to switch between bundled assets vs remote URL mode (and how to set/change the target URL). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a fuller Android build guide (CI-friendly) covering: environment setup, build/sync steps, debug APK build, optional release keystore/signing flow, artifact paths, install/share guidance, and troubleshooting (blank screens, routing, Internet Identity navigation). Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
